learning_objective: Students correlate the locations of environmental hazards with demographic data to identify patterns of environmental injustice.
specification:
Type: map **sim-id:** ej-mapping
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Level:** Analyze **Bloom Verb:** Correlate **Learning Objective:** Students correlate the locations of environmental hazards with demographic data to identify patterns of environmental injustice. **Instructional Rationale:** Map-based exploration makes abstract inequality visible and personal, connecting environmental science to social justice in a way that pure text cannot. **Visual Elements:** - Simplified map of a fictional city divided into districts - Color-coded overlay layers: - Pollution sources (factories, highways, waste sites) as red markers - Income levels (gradient from dark=low to light=high) - Health outcomes (asthma rates, cancer rates) as proportional circles - Green space access as green shading - Data panel showing statistics for selected district - Legend showing layer controls **Interactions:** - Toggle layers on/off to see correlations visually - Click districts to see detailed demographics and environmental data - "Propose policy" tool: place new facilities or green spaces and see impact predictions - Compare districts side by side - "Flip perspective" button: view from different stakeholder perspectives (resident, developer, city planner, environmental advocate) - Discussion prompts appear based on what patterns the student discovers **Colors:** Pollution: red (#e63946), low income: dark orange (#e76f51), high income: light blue (#a8dadc), health risk: purple (#7b2cbf), green space: green (#2a9d8f), neutral: gray (#adb5bd)
